markets.question: Indian Premier League: Rajasthan Royals vs Gujarat Titans
markets.description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Rajasthan Royals and Gujarat Titans scheduled for May 9 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rajasthan Royals hold home advantage at Sawai Mansingh Stadium in Jaipur for their IPL 2026 clash with Gujarat Titans on May 9, where the balanced pitch favors batsmen early but aids spinners later, emphasizing strike rotation and square boundaries. RR captain Riyan Parag's hamstring tweak from the May 1 loss to Delhi Capitals raises fitness concerns, though he expects recovery with a week's rest; his leadership proved vital in April's thriller win defending 210 against GT. GT seek revenge amid playoff push, bolstered by recent momentum versus Punjab Kings, but middle-order inconsistencies persist. Head-to-head tilts GT's way overall (6-2), yet RR's strong NRR and form in high-scoring chases shape trader consensus on a tight contest.

markets.question: Mansouri vs. Bolangi: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Skander Mansouri and Bienvenue Bolangi in the Brazzaville, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 3:00PM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger results.

events.description:

This market will resolve to 'Skander Mansouri' if Skander Mansouri advances against Bienvenue Bolangi.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

eventMetadata.context_description: Skander Mansouri, ranked around No. 839 with a career-high of 238, holds a significant experience edge over unranked qualifier Bienvenue Bolangi in their first-round Brazzaville Challenger clash on outdoor clay. Bolangi earned his spot with a decisive 6-1, 6-3 qualy win over local wild card Cheik Pandzou Ekoume on May 3, gaining momentum and home-crowd support in Congo. Mansouri, however, arrives off a recent qualy loss to Oscar Weightman in Abidjan 2 Challenger last week on hard courts, where his clay form remains key—historically solid in lower-tier events. No head-to-head exists; trader consensus reflects Mansouri's superior rankings, draw position, and stylistic matchup advantages, though Bolangi's fresh qualifier run and local conditions introduce upset potential via fatigue or weather shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Santos first-round clash on clay courts, Hernan Casanova holds a slight edge over home hopeful Mateus Alves in their 1-1 head-to-head, having prevailed in their most recent encounter last May in Bogota via a grueling three-setter (2-6, 7-6, 7-5). Currently ranked No. 420 to Alves' No. 548, the 32-year-old Argentine veteran leverages superior recent clay form despite a fresh ITF loss, while 25-year-old Alves seeks momentum post-Tallahassee defeat. With no reported injuries or withdrawals, trader sentiment reflects a tight contest shaped by baseline prowess, surface affinity, and Alves' crowd advantage in this Brazilian swing opener.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Brazzaville first-round matchup on red clay, higher-ranked Bor Artnak (No. 443) holds a trader consensus edge over Matyas Cerny (No. 699) in their debut head-to-head, reflecting Artnak's stronger 11-8 YTD record and career 97-57 clay mark despite a recent 4-3 retirement against Blaise Bicknell in Abidjan last week raising minor fitness concerns. Cerny enters with a 9-6 season (78-60 lifetime clay) off a qualifying loss to Rudolf Molleker in Ostrava, showing competitive form but lacking Artnak's momentum from an M25 Las Vegas semifinal. Artnak's 15cm height advantage could aid serve dominance outdoors, though Cerny's steady baseline game keeps it close; no withdrawals or weather delays reported ahead of today's Court 1 start.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both VfB eSports and G2 NORD each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Prime League 1st Division 2026 Spring Group B, G2 NORD holds a mid-table position at 1-1 series record (2-3 games, 8 points), bolstered by a recent series win over E WIE EINFACH E-SPORTS, though they fell 1-2 to leaders Eintracht Spandau on April 27. markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both GLORE and devils.one each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Skander Mansouri, ranked around No. 839 with a career-high of 238, holds a significant experience edge over unranked qualifier Bienvenue Bolangi in their first-round Brazzaville Challenger clash on outdoor clay. Bolangi earned his spot with a decisive 6-1, 6-3 qualy win over local wild card Cheik Pandzou Ekoume on May 3, gaining momentum and home-crowd support in Congo. Mansouri, however, arrives off a recent qualy loss to Oscar Weightman in Abidjan 2 Challenger last week on hard courts, where his clay form remains key—historically solid in lower-tier events. No head-to-head exists; trader consensus reflects Mansouri's superior rankings, draw position, and stylistic matchup advantages, though Bolangi's fresh qualifier run and local conditions introduce upset potential via fatigue or weather shifts.

markets.question: Will there be between 10 and 20 average daily transits of the Strait of Hormuz on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz that IMF Portwatch reports for April 30, 2026.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data for the specified date has been published. If no data for the specified date has been published by May 14, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on data for the most recent date prior to April 30, 2026, for which data is available.

This market will resolve based on the first publication of data for April 30, 2026. Any subsequent revisions will not be considered.
If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors 0-10 ships as the average daily transits through the Strait of Hormuz at end-April 2026, reflecting persistent dual blockades amid the US-Iran war. Shipping trackers like Kpler and Lloyd's List report just 191 vessels crossing the entire month—averaging under seven per day—with late April seeing 4-12 daily amid US naval enforcement turning back over 50 ships and Iranian seizures of non-compliant vessels. Failed ceasefire extensions and mutual restrictions on Iran-linked traffic sustained the near-standstill, down 93% from pre-war norms of 100+ vessels daily. A sudden diplomatic breakthrough or successful US Project Freedom escorts could challenge this, but no such de-escalation materialized by April 30.

markets.question: Will there be exactly 4 earthquakes of magnitude 5.5 or higher worldwide from May 4 - 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 5.5 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between May 4, 2026, 12:00 AM ET, and May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program, with the minimum magnitude set to 5.5 and the date parameters set to the relevant dates for this market's timeframe (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/search/).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

This market may not resolve until the timeframe of this market has concluded. If a qualifying earthquake has been recorded on the final day, this market may remain open for 24 hours to allow for revisions to the recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors more than nine magnitude 5.5 or greater earthquakes from May 4–10 (87% implied probability), aligned with USGS historical global averages of roughly 10 such events per week, driven by ongoing tectonic plate interactions along subduction zones like the Pacific Ring of Fire and transform faults. This baseline follows the Gutenberg-Richter frequency-magnitude distribution, where M5–5.9 events average 1,600 annually worldwide. As of May 6, USGS catalog records at least two M5.8 quakes off New Zealand on May 5, setting a brisk early pace with four days left; seismic clustering could accelerate totals, though quiet periods introduce uncertainty. Hourly USGS updates will refine tracking toward the May 10 resolution.

HRTS Academy mirrors the dominance with 2-0 wins over Falke (Apr 29) and GIANTX iTero, despite jungler Daglas' promotion to the main roster on Apr 15, leaning on Papiteero top, Mercy9 mid, Lure bot, and Batuuu support. MKF holds the edge from their 2-0 playoff win over HRTS in the LES Kick-off Tournament final run.

eventMetadata.context_description: Yankees rookie Cam Schlittler leads trader consensus at 27% implied probability for the 2026 AL Cy Young after a dominant start, posting a 1.51 ERA, 4-1 record, and 49 strikeouts over 47.1 innings while topping AL rankings in xERA, xFIP, K/BB ratio, and fWAR through eight starts, capped by six scoreless innings versus Texas on Tuesday. Teammate Max Fried trails at 13% with a 2.39 ERA and AL-leading 52.2 innings pitched in eight outings, bolstering his ace pedigree amid strong run support. Angels' Jose Soriano holds third at 10% behind a 1.74 ERA and 5-2 mark, leveraging elite velocity, though higher walks temper his peripherals in this wide-open early-season field defined by prospect breakouts and Tarik Skubal's recent injury.

markets.question: Will the Bank of Mexico announce an increase at the June meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the overnight interbank interest rate as a result of the monetary policy decision of the Bank of Mexico's June 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Mexico after its policy meeting scheduled for June 25, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of Mexico calendar: https://www.banxico.org.mx/viewers2/JSP/calendarioDifusion_es.jsp

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Mexico's statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 84% implied probability to no change in Banco de México's (Banxico) target overnight interbank rate at its June 2026 meeting, following the surprise 25 basis point cut to 6.75% on March 26 amid a divided Governing Board vote and despite persistent inflation above the 3% target. Early April 2026 headline CPI eased to 4.53% year-on-year from prior highs, signaling modest disinflation that supports a potential pause after the expected May 7 rate reduction priced at over 90% in parallel markets. Governor remarks on March 30 highlighted the easing cycle nearing completion, with core price pressures and rising global yields reinforcing trader caution for further adjustments. The May decision remains the pivotal catalyst ahead.

This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Brian Campbell, Aldrich Potgieter, Xander Schauffele, and Adam Scott at 50.5% implied probabilities to win the Truist Championship at Quail Hollow Club, reflecting a tightly contested signature event field with no dominant favorite amid parity in driving distance, strokes gained: approach, and course fit on the demanding par-71 layout. Campbell and Potgieter enter with fresh momentum from their recent Mexico Open playoff—where Campbell claimed his first PGA Tour victory—boosting sentiment in the past week, while Schauffele's elite iron play and Scott's steady ball-striking match the venue's emphasis on accuracy off the tee. World No. 2 Rory McIlroy's return post-Masters win and strong Quail Hollow history (multiple victories nearby) adds pressure, but the top-50-heavy field, finalized May 1, keeps odds bunched as historical upsets and variable greens in regulation create upset potential for the May 6-10 event.

markets.question: Will no listed leader be out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first listed individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Next leader out of power before 2027? (No Orban)
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the first listed individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Colombia President Gustavo Petro as the frontrunner at 56% to exit power first before 2027, anchored by his four-year term concluding August 7, 2026, under strict no-reelection rules, marking the earliest fixed departure among options. Recent cabinet reshuffles in February 2025 and persistently low approval ratings amid stalled reforms reinforce expectations of a routine handoff after Colombia's 2026 presidential election. Cuba President Miguel Díaz-Canel's 22% reflects heightened pressure from U.S. demands for his resignation in March 2026 amid economic crisis and oil sanctions, despite his defiant April 10 NBC interview vowing no step down. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer at 12% tracks dismal polls, with an April survey showing two-thirds of voters seeking his immediate resignation over leadership missteps. Lower odds for France's Emmanuel Macron and Turkey's Recep Tayyip Erdoğan cite lingering parliamentary chaos and unconfirmed health rumors, respectively, as barriers to early exits before Petro's timeline.

markets.question: Will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit (LOW) $540 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Meta Platforms' (META) share price trades around $601 as of May 5, 2026, down from a May 4 close of $610 amid post-earnings consolidation. Q1 2026 results showed robust revenue of $56.3 billion, up 33% year-over-year on AI-enhanced ad performance and Reels engagement, with adjusted EPS of $7.31 beating estimates. However, trader sentiment soured on elevated 2026 capital expenditure guidance of $125-145 billion for AI infrastructure, prompting a 6-7% after-hours drop. Analyst consensus targets average $840, signaling upside potential, while May 20 layoffs targeting 10% of workforce pose near-term risks ahead of Q2 reporting in late July. Prediction markets reflect caution on spending headwinds offsetting core ad revenue momentum.

This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Brian Campbell, Aldrich Potgieter, Xander Schauffele, and Adam Scott at 50.5% implied probabilities to win the Truist Championship at Quail Hollow Club, reflecting a tightly contested signature event field with no dominant favorite amid parity in driving distance, strokes gained: approach, and course fit on the demanding par-71 layout. Campbell and Potgieter enter with fresh momentum from their recent Mexico Open playoff—where Campbell claimed his first PGA Tour victory—boosting sentiment in the past week, while Schauffele's elite iron play and Scott's steady ball-striking match the venue's emphasis on accuracy off the tee. World No. 2 Rory McIlroy's return post-Masters win and strong Quail Hollow history (multiple victories nearby) adds pressure, but the top-50-heavy field, finalized May 1, keeps odds bunched as historical upsets and variable greens in regulation create upset potential for the May 6-10 event.

markets.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be between $72,000 and $74,000 on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Imperial Academy entered the United21 Season 47 playoffs as undefeated Group A winners with dominant Bo3 victories over LPH Gaming and Sangal ALTERS, showcasing strong map control on Inferno and Dust2. Infinite Gaming advanced as Group D runners-up after a decider win over AaB esport, building on their United21 Season 46 grand final triumph against BIG Academy. Stable rosters—Imperial featuring ta9z, sh1Fu, Kragh, aZe, and hyskeee; Infinite with mhN1, kreaz, Dytor, sl3nd, and Blytz—faced no reported changes or stand-ins. Imperial pulled off a 2-1 upset in Upper Bracket Round 1 on April 9, advancing further in the double-elimination bracket amid a competitive C-Tier field.

markets.question: Will Charles Leclerc win the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the winner of the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 24, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially listed in first place in the Final Classification published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race. The Final Classification is typically released 30-60 minutes after the race ends and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the Final Classification will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA's published classification will be used to resolve this market.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

events.description:

The Final Classification is typically released 30-60 minutes after the race ends and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the Final Classification will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA's published classification will be used to resolve this market.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes teammates Kimi Antonelli and George Russell dominate trader consensus at 31.5% and 27.5% implied probabilities for the Canadian Grand Prix winner, driven by their 1-2 championship standings after four rounds and Mercedes' early 2026 power unit edge under new regulations. Antonelli's blistering three-race winning streak—capped by his Miami GP triumph extending a 20-point lead—fuels his slight favoritism, while Russell's consistent qualifying strength keeps him close amid intra-team rivalry. Lando Norris lurks at 17.5% on McLaren's recent podium form and FP2 pace, with Max Verstappen at 10% reflecting Red Bull's struggles; tight gaps underscore Circuit Gilles Villeneuve's demands for pole position, tire strategy, and wall-dodging racecraft where upsets via DNFs remain viable.

markets.question: Will Trump speak to Mohammed bin Salman in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual speaks with Donald Trump between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Speaking is defined as any verbal interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be credible media reporting. However, in the absence of definitive or in the case of contradictory media reporting, statements by either of the specified individuals or their official representatives will also be considered.

Statements by the specified individuals or their official representatives will only be considered conclusive if the claims explicitly, or in context unambiguously, indicate that a qualifying talk occurred within the specified timeframe and took place via verbal communication, and provided that such claims are not contradicted by the other specified individual, their representatives, or credible media reporting by the end of the third calendar date (ET) following the statement in question.

If the date/time of a qualifying talk cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day following this market's above-specified timeframe, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether it was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Brazilian President Lula da Silva at 99% and Chinese President Xi Jinping at 96% for verbal interactions with President Trump in May 2026, driven by confirmed announcements: a bilateral summit with Xi in Beijing on May 14-15, rescheduled from April amid U.S.-Iran escalations but enabled by the May 5 Hormuz Strait operations pause to prioritize ceasefire talks, following Trump's May 4 remarks praising Xi. Lula's near-certainty reflects reported high-level coordination. Strong odds for NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte (78%) and French President Emmanuel Macron (77%) stem from ongoing alliance diplomacy, while German Chancellor Friedrich Merz sits at 68% amid EU talks. No May interactions confirmed post-May 1; Trump's May 6 schedule lacks specified bilaterals, with potential Iran deal reshaping priorities before month-end resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic field gained a significant boost three days ago with five-time major champion Brooks Koepka's late commitment, intensifying competition for top-20 finishes at Dunes Golf and Beach Club (par 71, 7,347 yards) during the May 7-10 event opposite the PGA Championship. This opposite-field tournament features 120 players, including PGA Tour regulars like Tom Kim, Billy Horschel, and recent winners Davis Riley and Webb Simpson, alongside Korn Ferry Tour standouts such as Chandler Blanchet and Neal Shipley vying for status. Limited course history favors strong ball-strikers amid potential coastal winds, while Andrew Putnam's withdrawal slightly softens the mid-tier; trader consensus reflects Koepka's recent T12 at the Masters elevating implied probabilities for established contenders over longshots.

markets.question: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Escalating US-Cuba tensions, fueled by President Trump's recent threats of military takeover and an oil blockade imposed in early 2026 that has plunged Cuba into energy blackouts, have pushed "Yes" odds to near parity, reflecting trader consensus on potential confrontation amid Cuban hosting of Russian and Chinese intelligence assets. Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez's May 5 denunciation of US military posturing as "cynical" and President Díaz-Canel's vows of readiness underscore mutual escalation signals, including Pentagon contingency planning reported in April and Flex 2026 exercises off Cuba's coast. However, the absence of direct naval, airspace, or ground incidents—despite a February speedboat clash—keeps "No" marginally ahead at 53.5%, with de-escalation possible via diplomacy or US focus on Iran; a triggering provocation like Russian troop deployments could swiftly tip toward "Yes."

markets.question: Will Jake Mitchell win the 2026 Watford mayoral election?
markets.description:
The 2026 Watford mayoral election is currently scheduled to be held on May 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next mayor of Watford as a result of this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by April 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the Watford Borough Council.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Liberal Democrat Peter Taylor commands 99.7% trader consensus in the Watford mayoral election set for May 7 under first-past-the-post voting, reflecting his strong track record since 2018 and the party's 21-year dominance, including 30 of 36 council seats after sweeping 11 of 12 wards in 2024 locals plus recent by-election holds. Challengers—Labour's Keith Morgan (26.5% in 2022), Conservative Abdul Laskar (18.7%), and minor candidates from Green, Reform UK, Independent, and UK Voice—mirror prior weak fields amid Lib Dem control. While highly improbable, an overnight scandal, voter turnout surge, or post-election recount could theoretically shift the outcome before council abolition in 2027.

markets.question: US-Iran nuclear deal before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if an official agreement over Iranian nuclear research and/or nuclear weapon development, defined as a publicly announced mutual agreement, is reached between the United States and Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

Agreements that include the United States and Iran as parties, even if they also involve other countries (e.g., a multilateral deal like the JCPOA), will qualify for resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by the United States and/or the Islamic Republic of Iran, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

events.title: US-Iran nuclear deal before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if an official agreement over Iranian nuclear research and/or nuclear weapon development, defined as a publicly announced mutual agreement, is reached between the United States and Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

Agreements that include the United States and Iran as parties, even if they also involve other countries (e.g., a multilateral deal like the JCPOA), will qualify for resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by the United States and/or the Islamic Republic of Iran, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's May 6 announcement of "great progress" toward a US-Iran agreement, including a pause on naval operations in the Strait of Hormuz, has driven trader consensus to imply a 63.5% probability of a nuclear deal before 2027, reflecting Iran's recent softening on including its nuclear program in talks and a emerging 14-point framework. Key elements under discussion involve a 12-15 year freeze on uranium enrichment, enhanced IAEA inspections, phased sanctions relief, release of frozen Iranian assets, and de-escalation of Hormuz tensions, following multiple rounds mediated by Pakistan and Oman since April. Sticking points persist on enrichment duration and stockpile removal, with Iran's response expected within 48 hours potentially triggering comprehensive negotiations in Geneva or Islamabad, amid historical negotiation hurdles and risks of renewed escalation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aryna Sabalenka tops trader consensus at 32% implied probability for the 2026 Women's Wimbledon title, fueled by her sustained World No. 1 dominance through a 25-1 start to the year, including deep Madrid and Rome clay runs that highlight her power game transitioning well to grass. Elena Rybakina follows at 21.5%, bolstered by her 2022 Wimbledon triumph, massive serve suiting the surface, and recent Australian Open crown over Sabalenka plus a Stuttgart clay title narrowing the WTA Race gap. Iga Świątek's 17.5% reflects her No. 3 ranking despite grass-court limitations, with improved 2025 showings but clay focus. Amanda Anisimova's 7.8% stems from her career-best sixth ranking and hot streak, including Australian Open deep run, amid a wide-open field post-May 4 rankings.

markets.question: Will Trump reduce the deficit before 2027?
markets.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) reports a lower monthly deficit in December 2026 than in September 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source will be the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) published by the U.S. Department of the Treasury (fiscaldata.treasury.gov). The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 84.5% implied probability for "No," driven by Congressional Budget Office projections showing the fiscal year 2026 federal deficit at $1.9 trillion (5.8% of GDP), with year-to-date figures already exceeding $1 trillion through February amid persistent revenue shortfalls and spending pressures. President Trump's policy mix—extension of 2017 tax cuts adding $1.4 trillion to decade-long deficits, alongside 42% defense spending hikes to $1.5 trillion in the April FY2027 budget proposal—offsets proposed non-defense discretionary cuts, per CBO analysis. Recent tariff policy shifts could further widen shortfalls by $1.1 trillion over ten years, reinforcing fiscal expansion. Key catalysts include final FY2026 Treasury data by September and debt ceiling negotiations.

markets.question: Will "The Devil Wears Prada 2" 2nd Weekend Box Office be greater than 58m?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to how much "The Devil Wears Prada 2" Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its second weekend. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day weekend (May 8 - May 10) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

Strong word-of-mouth signals—A- CinemaScore surpassing the original's B, 88% Rotten Tomatoes audience score, and 78% critics' fresh rating—suggest potential for a shallow 30-35% drop, but front-loaded nostalgia and competition from Michael's second frame could pressure toward sub-$50 million. Mother's Day on May 10 boosts family outings for women-led fare, while light new releases favor holdovers; watch Friday presales and tracking updates for momentum shifts.

events.title: MLS: 2026 Goalkeeper of the Year
events.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Goalkeeper of the Year.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Luis Barraza of Inter Miami CF, Pedro Gallese (formerly Orlando City), San Jose Earthquakes' Daniel, and Chicago Fire's Chris Brady in a virtual deadlock around 49%, underscoring the wide-open 2026 MLS Goalkeeper of the Year race just one month into the 34-match regular season. Early leaders in saves include Brady and Daniel (17 each), high-volume Brad Stuver (32 for Austin FC), and clean sheet pacesetters like Hugo Lloris (8 for LAFC), but no one dominates goals against average or save percentage amid parity across metrics. Strong team defenses boost candidates like Kristijan Kahlina (Charlotte FC) and Jonathan Sirois (CF Montréal), while Gallese's free agency status fuels speculation of a return. Sustained shutouts, goals prevented, and playoff implications will decide the voter-decided award.

This market will resolve according to the first individual elected by the Parliament of Andalusia to serve as President of Andalusia following this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker Presidents will not count.

If no such President has been elected by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Andalusia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Juanma Moreno of the Partido Popular commands overwhelming trader consensus at 98% implied probability to remain President of the Junta de Andalucía following the May 17 regional election, driven by consistent polling averages over the past month showing his party securing an absolute majority of over 55 seats in the 109-seat Parliament of Andalusia. Recent surveys from Sigma Dos, Target Point, and CIS highlight Moreno's high approval as the top-valued leader, bolstered by vote gains from a declining Vox and stagnant PSOE under candidate María Jesús Montero, amid a campaign kickoff marked by debates and focus on housing and immigration. While structural incumbency and historical precedent favor continuity from his 2022 absolute majority win, low-probability shifts could arise from late scandals, voter turnout surges, or post-election coalition dynamics if polls tighten unexpectedly.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 78% implied probability against a Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, driven by the absence of a public S-1 filing or roadshow announcements since the company's confidential SEC submission in January 2026, leaving under 55 days for completion amid a compressed IPO window. Secondary market trading on platforms like Hiive values shares at $40.71 as of May 5—implying an ~$8.5 billion market cap—bolstering 14.8% odds for a sub-$15 billion debut if it occurs, while higher brackets languish below 2% due to monetization headwinds from a gamer-centric userbase, modest ~$725 million ARR growth, and SaaS valuation contraction from 2021 peaks. Key catalysts include potential public filing or regulatory feedback.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M as the narrow frontrunner at 49.5% implied probability to win Colombia's 2026 presidential election, closely trailed by leftist Iván Cepeda Castro (38.5%) and right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella (36.5%), with Paloma Valencia at 24.6%, signaling a fragmented first-round field unlikely to produce a majority ahead of the May 31 vote and potential June runoff. Recent Invamer polling from late April showed Cepeda surging to 44% amid Pacto Histórico's strong March congressional gains—securing the largest Senate bloc—but right-leaning challengers gained from voter backlash to a wave of pre-election violence and security fears. Markets diverge from poll averages emphasizing Cepeda's lead, betting on undecided voter shifts, endorsements, or consolidation in battleground areas as the campaign intensifies.

markets.question: Will Lando Norris get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the driver who achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a driver sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that driver.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a dead-even field for pole position at the Canadian Grand Prix, with all 22 drivers priced at 50% implied probability amid 2026's transformative active aero and sustainable power unit regulations that have compressed one-lap pace across the grid. Circuit Gilles Villeneuve's low-downforce demands for straight-line speed and braking stability favor versatile setups, but with no practice sessions yet and over three weeks until the May 22-24 weekend, no clear qualifying favorite has emerged to shift sentiment.

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Stefanos Tsitsipas vs Tomas Machac
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Stefanos Tsitsipas and Tomas Machac in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Stefanos Tsitsipas' if Stefanos Tsitsipas advances against Tomas Machac.

This market will resolve to 'Tomas Machac' if Tomas Machac advances against Stefanos Tsitsipas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stefanos Tsitsipas enters as the 63.5% implied probability favorite against Tomas Machac in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia Round 1, reflecting his superior clay-court pedigree and recent form resurgence despite a shaky start to the European swing. Tsitsipas, a three-time Monte Carlo Masters champion and past Rome finalist, notched a confidence-boosting third-round appearance in Madrid last week by defeating eighth seed Alexander Bublik 6-2, 7-5—his first top-20 clay win since the Paris Olympics—while declaring himself physically recovered from prior back issues. Machac, who stunned Tsitsipas in four sets at the Australian Open on hard courts (leveling their 1-1 head-to-head), showed clay promise by pushing Jannik Sinner to three sets in Monte Carlo's Round of 16 but holds a mixed 4-4 clay record this season. Traders price in Tsitsipas' surface expertise and Foro Italico experience amid a competitive matchup on outdoor red clay.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

The Republican nominee, John Elleson, emerged from a low-turnout primary with just 12,000 votes, underscoring limited GOP strength in this safe Democratic stronghold rated Solid D by Cook Political Report. With no major developments in the past 30 days and no general election polls yet, scenarios like a Biss scandal, massive Republican national wave, or independent Delila Barrera surge could shift odds, though historical precedents for such safe seats suggest stability ahead of the November 3 general election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket has locked in a 100% implied probability for a 32°C high temperature in Wuhan on May 6, 2026, driven by the China Meteorological Administration's (CMA) morning bulletin forecasting a maximum of exactly 32°C at Wuhan Tianhe International Airport amid overcast conditions and light southerly winds. This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 1240-1279 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy enters this BO3 matchup in the Exort Series Main Stage as the favored side following their 2-1 victory over Wave Esports three weeks ago in Exort Series #25, showcasing strong T-side execution on key maps like Ancient and Mirage. The academy squad, featuring young riflers Kiryasoo, kidofpain, mazay, s1nside, and VILBy under coach S0tF1k, maintains roster stability since February roster refresh, while Wave (#251 HLTV) relies on recent additions Katalic, sheezy, miksozzz, and Belof amid mixed March results including early exits. Trader consensus reflects Spirit's head-to-head edge and tier-2 momentum in the Swiss format, where top 8 advance to playoffs; watch map vetoes and pistol rounds in the current CS2 meta favoring aggressive entries.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 62.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 63 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls drive trader consensus favoring Senator Flávio Bolsonaro for second place in Brazil's October 4, 2026, presidential election first round, with Lula da Silva positioned as the likely leader. Ideia (May 1–5) shows Lula at 40% and Flávio at 36%, while Real Time Big Data (May 2–4) has Lula at 40% and Flávio at 34%; earlier April surveys from AtlasIntel, Datafolha, and Quaest similarly place Flávio 4–9 points behind Lula but ahead of all others like Ronaldo Caiado (3–6%) and Romeu Zema (3–4%). Flávio's consolidation of right-wing support amid Lula's steady but narrowing first-round leads reflects polarized dynamics, with no other candidate exceeding 6%, implying a probable Lula-Flávio runoff. Upcoming debates and regional shifts could influence undecided voters (9–19%).

markets.question: Spread: Manchester City FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Manchester City FC" if Manchester City FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Crystal Palace FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Manchester City FC vs. Crystal Palace FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's home Premier League clash with Crystal Palace, originally set for March 21 at Etihad Stadium, was postponed due to City's participation in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal the following day, with no rescheduled date confirmed yet—likely in late April or May midweeks. Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

markets.question: Will Reform UK win at least 2200 council seat elections in the 2026 United Kingdom local elections?
markets.description:
United Kingdom local elections are scheduled to be held on May 7, 2026. 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Reform UK wins at least the listed number of council seats for the United Kingdom's metropolitan boroughs, London borough councils, unitary authorities, county councils, and district councils as a result of this election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A candidate will be considered an official candidate of Reform UK if they are officially nominated by Reform UK and are registered for the relevant election in affiliation with Reform UK. Independent candidates will not count for any party.

Only elections for the United Kingdom’s metropolitan boroughs, London borough councils, unitary authorities, county councils, and district councils taking place on May 7, 2026, or rescheduled to within seven days thereafter, will be counted for this market.

Resolution of this market will be based on the results of the relevant elections, once those results are official. If the results of any of the relevant 2026 United Kingdom local elections aren’t known by April 30, 2027 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the available results up to that point. If none of the results of the relevant 2026 United Kingdom local elections are known by that time, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the respective council government.

markets.question: Will CZ post 140-159 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 325M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

These actions aim to stabilize domestic supply chains, with recent solicitations for up to 92.5 million barrels in additional SPR crude loans. Traders monitor weekly EIA reports through June 5 for further drawdowns, alongside potential DOE auctions, refinery demand, and OPEC+ output adjustments that could accelerate or halt declines. Upcoming bids and geopolitical developments remain key catalysts.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Shanghai be 20°C on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

No major developments have shifted conditions in the past week, per China Meteorological Administration monitoring, leaving traders focused on differentiating factors: northerly cold air advection and clear skies enabling radiative cooling to 12°C versus southerly warm flows, low clouds, or urban heat island effects trapping heat for 22°C+ minima. Historical data shows extremes rare below 12°C or above 22°C; updated model runs expected today could tip the balance before resolution.
